calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-30-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-28-2016 Set For Case Management Conference On Oct-19-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-31-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Wells Fargo Bank satisfied its burden of production by presenting admissible evidence that all of plaintiff Robert Yupangco's claims are barred by the one-year statute of limitations of CCP 340(c) and Mr. Yupangco did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue. (Mac v. Bank of America (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 562, 565; UMF 4, 6, 8.) Mr. Yupangco learned about the allegedly forged checks in November 2012 and had access to those checks no later than January 2013, but did not file this lawsuit until April 2, 2015. (UMF 4, 6, 8; Declaration of Ana Carcamo Ex. F.) Section 340(c) apply to all of Mr. Yupangco's claims because they are all based on the alleged forgery of the checks. (Roy Supply, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (1995) 39 Cal.App.4th 1051; Complaint 21-28.) Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar May-31-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues - Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. Bank of America satisfied its burden of production by presenting admissible evidence that all of plaintiff Robert Yupangco's claims are barred by the one-year statute of limitations of CCP 340(c) and Mr. Yupangco did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue. (Mac v. Bank of America (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 562, 565; UMF 4, 6, 8.) Mr. Yupangco learned about the allegedly forged checks in November 2012 and had access to those checks no later than January 2013, but did not file this lawsuit until April 2, 2015. (UMF 4, 6, 8; Declaration of Ana Carcamo Ex. F.) Section 340(c) apply to all of Mr. Yupangco's claims because they are all based on the alleged forgery of the checks. (Roy Supply, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (1995) 39 Cal.App.4th 1051; Complaint 21-28.) Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
